examples.title: Calculating MAE
examples.description: A pseudocode example of how to calculate the Mean Absolute Error (MAE) for a regression model.
examples.code:
FUNCTION calculate_mae(predicted_values, actual_values)
  mae = 0
  FOR i = 1 TO length(predicted_values)
    mae = mae + ABS(predicted_values[i] - actual_values[i])
  END FOR
  mae = mae / length(predicted_values)
  RETURN mae
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: This pseudocode calculates the MAE by summing the absolute differences between the predicted and actual values, and then dividing by the number of values.

examples.title: Calculating MSE
examples.description: A Python example of how to calculate the Mean Squared Error (MSE) for a regression model.
examples.code:
import numpy as np

def calculate_mse(predicted_values, actual_values):
  mse = np.mean((predicted_values - actual_values) ** 2)
  return mse
examples.explanation: This Python code calculates the MSE by taking the mean of the squared differences between the predicted and actual values.
